questions.prompt: Report this: He said, "I am tired."

options.text: He said that he is tired.
He said that he was tired.
He said that I was tired.
He said that he were tired.

questions.explanation: In reported speech the present simple backshifts to the past simple, and "I" becomes "he": he said that he was tired. (a) keeps the present without backshift; (c) keeps the wrong pronoun "I"; (d) uses "were" with a singular subject.

questions.prompt: Report this: She said, "I will call you tomorrow."

options.text: She said she will call me the next day.
She said she would call me the next day.
She said she would call you tomorrow.
She said she would called me the next day.

questions.explanation: "will" backshifts to "would", "you" becomes "me", and "tomorrow" becomes "the next day": she said she would call me the next day. (a) keeps "will"; (c) keeps "you"/"tomorrow" unchanged; (d) adds -ed after "would".

questions.prompt: Report this: "I bought a new phone yesterday," he said.

options.text: He said he bought a new phone yesterday.
He said he had bought a new phone the day before.
He said he has bought a new phone the day before.
He said he buys a new phone the previous day.

questions.explanation: The past simple backshifts to the past perfect, and "yesterday" becomes "the day before": he had bought a new phone the day before. (a) keeps the past simple and "yesterday"; (c) uses the present perfect, not the past perfect; (d) wrongly uses the present "buys".

questions.prompt: Report this question: "Where do you live?" she asked.

options.text: She asked where do I live.
She asked where did I live.
She asked where I lived.
She asked where I live?

questions.explanation: A reported wh-question uses statement word order (no auxiliary "do", no question mark) and backshifts the tense: she asked where I lived. (a) and (b) keep the auxiliary "do/did" and question order; (d) keeps the question mark and present tense.

questions.prompt: Report this command: "Open the window," the teacher told us.

options.text: The teacher told us open the window.
The teacher told us to open the window.
The teacher told us that open the window.
The teacher told us opening the window.

questions.explanation: Commands are reported with "tell + object + to + infinitive": the teacher told us to open the window. (a) drops the "to"; (c) wrongly uses "that" for a command; (d) uses the -ing form instead of the to-infinitive.

questions.prompt: Report this yes/no question: "Are you coming to the party?" he asked.

options.text: He asked if I was coming to the party.
He asked if I am coming to the party.
He asked was I coming to the party.
He asked if you were coming to the party.

questions.explanation: A reported yes/no question uses "if/whether", statement word order and backshift: he asked if I was coming. (b) fails to backshift "am" to "was"; (c) keeps question word order with no "if"; (d) keeps the wrong pronoun "you".
